meta.description: Vulnerability Database Router - lightweight entry point for agents
meta.architecture: Tiered search: router (this file) → manifests (DB/manifests/*.json) → vulnerability files (DB/**/*.md)
meta.usage: 1) Identify category from protocolContext or keywords. 2) Load the relevant manifest. 3) Find specific patterns with line ranges. 4) Read only those lines from the .md file.

searchStrategy.description: How agents should search this database for maximum precision

steps.detail: Use protocolContext to match the protocol type being audited, OR use quickKeywords to find the right category
Read DB/manifests/<category>.json (much smaller than this file). Each manifest has pattern-level entries with exact line ranges.
Search manifest patterns by title, severity, codeKeywords. Each pattern has lineStart/lineEnd.
Use read_file with lineStart/lineEnd to read ONLY the relevant section. Do NOT read entire files.

protocolContext.description: Map protocol type → relevant manifests + priority patterns. Load the manifest file, then search for patterns matching your codebase.

token_launch.description: New token launches, meme coins, trading contracts

quickKeywords.description: Keyword → pattern-level references. Each entry points to a specific pattern with lineStart/lineEnd for direct file access. Only keywords with ≤10 matches included (specific enough to be useful).

auditChecklist.description: Quick checklist items organized by vulnerability type
general: Check all external calls for reentrancy
Verify slippage protection on all swaps
Check oracle staleness and manipulation vectors
Verify access control on sensitive functions
Check for first depositor/inflation attacks on vaults
Verify rounding direction favors the protocol
